architecture.summary: The system takes a trained neural network and induces human-readable logic-based theories by establishing mappings from neuron activations to human-defined concepts. The induced theories are intended to faithfully represent the classification process of the original network. Information flows from neuron activations through concept mappings to produce symbolic logic rules.

biblio.title: Looking Inside the Black-Box: Logic-based Explanations for Neural Networks

claims.statement: The developed procedure induces human-understandable logic-based theories that attempt to represent the classification process of a given neural network model by establishing mappings from neuron activation values to human-defined concepts
The theories developed through the method are faithful to the representations learned by the neural networks that they are built to describe
Empirical results assess the quality of the developed theories for different neural network models on a synthetic image classification task
